mission: Use UnisonAI as a pressure source for local-engine memory, teacher-exit, verification, Discord-interface, and zero/low-parameter benchmark discipline.

sources.title: MettaMazza/UnisonAI README
observed_claims: UnisonAI presents itself as a zero-trained-parameter engine where memory is written as counted corpus state rather than learned by gradient descent.
It uses y/n closure, permanent corrections, an anti-ledger for rejected answers, self-play, and an autonomous teacher/tutor loop.
It reports fixed-item MMLU-style comparisons against local models and separate end-to-end verification checks.
Its Discord face is described as an interface over one live engine, scoped to a configured channel rather than a whole server.
It emphasizes readable persistent state, logs, run artifacts, and tool traces.
benchmark_implications: Local-engine benchmarks should measure correction permanence, negative-ledger behavior, and teacher-retirement evidence.
Claims of zero/low-parameter advantage must be separated from trained-model comparison claims and tied to fixed-item artifacts.
Discord interfaces should be scored for scope lock, interface/engine separation, secret handling, and receipt completeness.
Tool-use agents should be penalized when they narrate intent without performing the promised action.
Readable memory and logs should become release evidence rather than marketing prose.

benchmark_lanes.description: Measure whether a local engine can learn from corrections and retire helper/teacher models by territory without self-reinforcing unverified answers.
scenario_families: negative feedback withholds prior bad answer
teacher answer is held after a loss
self-play checks held lessons without reinforcing misses
territory graduation is counted rather than scheduled

benchmark_lanes.description: Pressure claims about zero/low-parameter local engines with fixed-item scorecards, exact-count traceability, negative results, and explicit limits.
Score a Discord-facing agent interface for ReAct follow-through, engine/interface separation, channel scope lock, and safe trace receipts.
